part.text: I’ll locate the target-side PNG decode size check and compare it only against the provided source hunk, then apply the minimal removal if the same premature cleanup exists.

input.pattern: CommonFreeDecode\(&decode\)|PNG size too large for int|class DecodeImageV2Op

state.title: CommonFreeDecode\(&decode\)|PNG size too large for int|class DecodeImageV2Op

part.text: The target code has the same invalid-size branch and the same `png::CommonFreeDecode(&decode)` call before `OP_REQUIRES`. I’m removing only that cleanup call so error handling does not use freed decoder state.
The hunk applies cleanly to the target base. I’m applying the same minimal change in the workspace now.

input.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: tensorflow/core/kernels/image/decode_image_op.cc
@@
     if (width != static_cast<int64>(decode.width) || width <= 0 ||
         width >= (1LL << 27) || height != static_cast<int64>(decode.height) ||
         height <= 0 || height >= (1LL << 27) || total_size >= (1LL << 29)) {
-      png::CommonFreeDecode(&decode);
       OP_REQUIRES(context, false,
                   errors::InvalidArgument("PNG size too large for int: ",
                                           decode.width, " by ", decode.height));
*** End Patch

state.title: git diff -- tensorflow/core/kernels/image/decode_image_op.cc

part.text:
```yaml
backport_result:
  status: success
  patch: |
    diff --git a/tensorflow/core/kernels/image/decode_image_op.cc b/tensorflow/core/kernels/image/decode_image_op.cc
    index 61b126fb81..4398378d13 100644
    --- a/tensorflow/core/kernels/image/decode_image_op.cc
    +++ b/tensorflow/core/kernels/image/decode_image_op.cc
    @@ -339,7 +339,6 @@ class DecodeImageV2Op : public OpKernel {
         if (width != static_cast<int64>(decode.width) || width <= 0 ||
             width >= (1LL << 27) || height != static_cast<int64>(decode.height) ||
             height <= 0 || height >= (1LL << 27) || total_size >= (1LL << 29)) {
    -      png::CommonFreeDecode(&decode);
           OP_REQUIRES(context, false,
                       errors::InvalidArgument("PNG size too large for int: ",
                                               decode.width, " by ", decode.height));
  notes: >
    Removed the premature PNG decoder cleanup before constructing the invalid-size error, matching the security fix intent.
```
